findings.message: The ADR requires `payload_types` entries to be complete type strings, including generic arguments, but both renderer sections describe resolving each `payload_types` value as a type token through `type_index`. For payloads like `Result<ProposedDecision, DomainError>` or `Vec<ProposedDecision>`, the declared value will not match a catalogue type name directly, so the planned edge generation would miss the inner payload types while still requiring authors to write the generic-complete form. Clarify that renderers must extract type-name tokens from each payload type string before resolving, or constrain `payload_types` to catalogue type names only.

`T002` and `T003` both suppress `dst_id == src_id` / self-loops for variant payload edges. That drops legitimate recursive enum payloads, so cases like `enum Node { Next(Node) }` or `Box<Node>` will lose a real enum→payload edge in both Contract Map and Reality View. The new feature therefore cannot represent one of the most important payload shapes it is supposed to expose.

`T002` and `T003` suppress variant-payload self-loops, but the spec requires enum -> payload type edges for resolved payload types. Recursive payloads such as `enum Node { Next(Box<Node>) }` resolve back to the enum itself, so the plan would drop a legitimate payload edge in both renderers and fail the stated representation semantics. Remove that suppression for variant-payload edges or explicitly exclude recursive payloads from the spec.

`OS-03` cites `knowledge/adr/2026-04-28-0135-tddd-struct-kind-uniformization-and-catalogue-linter.md#OS-04`, but the referenced ADR does not define an `OS-04` design decision/section for this rationale. The out-of-scope boundary is therefore not traceable from the SSoT citation.

`EnumVariantDeclaration` is described by the spec and implementation plan as a new value object to add, but its catalogue entry has no `action` field, so the generated view renders the action as `—` instead of `add`. That contradicts T001/IN-01 and weakens the plan's signal semantics for this new type; declare it with `"action": "add"` to match the rest of the plan-artifact intent.

`build_type_graph` is described as both consuming a `SchemaExport` and extracting payload data from rustdoc JSON. The implementation plan assigns the actual rustdoc extraction to `schema_export.rs::extract_enum_variants()`, so this catalogue entry/spec reference puts the responsibility on the wrong component and leaves IN-07/AC-02 semantically inconsistent for implementers.

The plan says `variant_payload` should render with `"-->"` as the same arrowhead as a field edge, and CN-04 also documents field edges as `-->|.field_name|`. Existing TypeGraph field-edge semantics are solid no-arrow containment (`---|field_name|`), so following this plan would make the new Reality View variant edge inconsistent with the stated field-edge comparison and likely change the intended visual semantics.

IN-07 assigns rustdoc JSON payload extraction to `build_type_graph`, but the implementation plan assigns that extraction to `schema_export.rs::extract_enum_variants()` and says `build_type_graph` merely receives payload-aware declarations; `infrastructure-types.json` likewise says `build_type_graph` propagates `SchemaExport` values without re-parsing rustdoc JSON. This leaves the responsibility boundary contradictory for T001/AC-02/CN-06.

CN-04 describes the normal field edge as directed (`-->|.field_name|`), and the S3 narrative says `variant_payload` should use the same `-->` arrowhead as field edges, but T003 then says the new arm is `-->|label|` “distinct from field edges which use `---|label|`”. The plan is internally inconsistent about whether Reality View field edges are directed or undirected, so an implementer cannot tell which edge semantics to preserve.
